name: Acre (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: wealth, especially mineral and rubber wealth
the blood shed by the Acrean revolutionaries

description: A field divided diagonally from the upper hoist to the lower fly, yellow above and green below, with a single red five-pointed star in the upper-hoist corner.
history: Based on the flag of the Republic of Acre proclaimed by Luis Galvez in 1899 and adapted by the revolutionaries of Placido de Castro; adopted as the flag of the Federal Territory of Acre in 1921 and confirmed for the State of Acre after its elevation to statehood in 1962.

features.symbolism: Diagonally divided yellow (upper hoist) and green (lower fly), inherited from the flag of the short-lived independent Republic of Acre proclaimed against Bolivia in 1899
the Acrean Revolution led by Placido de Castro that secured the territory for Brazil
